thoughts.subject: Defining CSS Styles
thoughts.description:
\n\n

I've begun outlining the `App.css` file for the weather site. My current focus is implementing a modern glassmorphism design. I'm prioritizing transparency, subtle gradients, and blurred backgrounds to achieve that sleek look. I'm working to balance visual appeal and readability.

args.content:
/* Global Styles: Basic resets and high-end typography setup */
:root { /* Define global color variables and blur levels */
  --bg-image: url('https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1534088568595-a066f710b81f?auto=format&fit=crop&q=80&w=2000'); /* High-quality atmospheric background */
  --glass-bg: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05); /* Ultra-light translucent background for glass effect */
  --glass-border: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.15); /* Soft border to define glass edges */
  --accent: #00d2ff; /* Primary accent color for highlights and icons */
  --text: #ffffff; /* Default text color for dark background */
} /* End of root variables */

* { /* Apply box-sizing reset to all elements */
  margin: 0; /* Remove default browser margins */
  padding: 0; /* Remove default browser padding */
  box-sizing: border-box; /* Ensure padding doesn't affect element width */
  font-family: 'Inter', -apple-system, sans-serif; /* Use modern, clean sans-serif font stack */
} /* End of universal reset */

body { /* Main body configuration for the full-screen experience */
  background: var(--bg-image) no-repeat center center fixed; /* Apply the fixed atmospheric background */
  background-size: cover; /* Ensure background scales to fill the viewport */
  color: var(--text); /* Set default typography color */
  min-height: 100vh; /* Ensure body takes up at least the full viewport height */
  overflow-x: hidden; /* Prevent horizontal scrollbars during animations */
} /* End of body styles */

/* Application Root Layout: Flexbox container for full-page centering */
.dashboard-root { /* Main wrapper for the entire dashboard UI */
  padding: 40px; /* Internal spacing from screen edges */
  display: flex; /* Use flexbox for vertical orientation */
  flex-direction: column; /* Stack header and main content vertically */
  gap: 30px; /* Consistent spacing between layout blocks */
  max-width: 1400px; /* Limit dashboard width on large monitors */
  margin: 0 auto; /* Center the dashboard horizontally */
} /* End of root container */

/* Glass Header Styles: Translucent navigation bar with search */
.glass-header { /* Header container with glassmorphism properties */
  display: flex; /* Flexbox for logo and search bar alignment */
  justify-content: space-between; /* Push logo and search to opposite ends */
  align-items: center; /* Vertically center header content */
  padding: 20px 30px; /* Internal padding for header content */
  background: var(--glass-bg); /* Semi-transparent background */
  backdrop-filter: blur(20px); /* Apply sophisticated background blur */
  -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(20px); /* Safari support for backdrop blur */
  border-radius: 20px; /* Rounded corners for a modern look */
  border: 1px solid var(--glass-border); /* Subtle edge definition */
} /* End of header styles */

.logo-text { /* Application branding text styling */
  font-size: 24px; /* Medium-large font size for prominence */
  font-weight: 800; /* Extra bold weight for brand impact */
  letter-spacing: -1px; /* Tighter letter spacing for a sleek look */
  background: linear-gradient(to right, #ffffff, var(--accent)); /* Gradient text effect */
  -webkit-background-clip: text; /* Clip gradient to the text glyphs */
  -webkit-text-fill-color: transparent; /* Make original text color transparent */
} /* End of logo styles */

/* Search Bar Components: Modern inputs and glass buttons */
.search-bar { /* Form container for location input */
  display: flex; /* Inline layout for input and button */
  gap: 10px; /* Spacing between input and search button */
} /* End of search bar container */

.glass-input { /* Style for the city lookup text field */
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); /* Slightly more opaque background for readability */
  border: 1px solid var(--glass-border); /* Subtle border for input box */
  padding: 12px 20px; /* Internal spacing for typed text */
  border-radius: 12px; /* Smooth rounded corners */
  color: white; /* Typed text color */
  outline: none; /* Remove default browser focus ring */
  width: 250px; /* Fixed width for consistent alignment */
} /* End of input styles */

.glass-button { /* Style for the update/submit button */
  background: var(--accent); /* Solid accent color for call-to-action */
  color: #000; /* Dark text for contrast against bright accent */
  border: none; /* Remove default button border */
  padding: 12px 24px; /* Internal padding for button tap area */
  border-radius: 12px; /* Match input corner radius */
  font-weight: 700; /* Bold text for clarity */
  cursor: pointer; /* Change cursor to pointer on hover */
  transition: transform 0.2s; /* Smooth hover interaction effect */
} /* End of button styles */

.glass-button:hover { transform: scale(1.05); } /* Slightly enlarge button on hover */

/* Bento Grid System: Modern masonry-style layout for cards */
.bento-grid { /* Main container for all information cards */
  display: grid; /* Enable CSS Grid layout */
  grid-template-columns: repeat(12, 1fr); /* 12-column system for maximum flexibility */
  grid-template-rows: auto auto; /* Two primary rows of information */
  gap: 20px; /* Consistent spacing between grid items */
} /* End of grid container */

/* Universal Glass Card Properties: Base styling for all dash modules */
.glass-card { /* Shared properties for every dashboard card */
  background: var(--glass-bg); /* Translucent backdrop */
  backdrop-filter: blur(15px); /* Moderate blur for readability */
  -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(15px); /* Safari blur support */
  border-radius: 24px; /* Large, modern corner radii */
  border: 1px solid var(--glass-border); /* Consistent subtle borders */
  padding: 30px; /* Standard internal card padding */
  transition: background 0.3s; /* Smooth transition for interaction states */
} /* End of shared card styles */

.section-title { /* Heading styles for card sub-sections */
  font-size: 14px; /* Small heading for subtle information hierarchy */
  text-transform: uppercase; /* Uppercase for a technical label feel */
  letter-spacing: 2px; /* Wide spacing for better readability at small sizes */
  margin-bottom: 20px; /* Space between title and card content */
  opacity: 0.6; /* Reduced opacity to emphasize data over labels */
} /* End of section title styles */

/* Main Weather Card: Feature module for current conditions */
.main-weather-card { /* Highlighted card for current weather */
  grid-column: span 8; /* Occupy two-thirds of the grid width */
  display: flex; /* Flex layout for hero and stats */
  flex-direction: column; /* Stack hero and stats vertically */
  justify-content: space-between; /* Maximize vertical space usage */
} /* End of main card styles */

.weather-hero { /* High-impact area for temp and icon */
  display: flex; /* Align icon and temperature horizontally */
  align-items: center; /* Center items vertically */
  gap: 40px; /* Wide gap for clear visual separation */
} /* End of hero container */

.big-icon { font-size: 80px; } /* Extra large emoji icon for current weather */

.current-temp { /* Large temperature display styling */
  font-size: 100px; /* Massive size for immediate readability */
  font-weight: 800; /* Extra heavy weight */
  letter-spacing: -4px; /* Tight tracking for high-impact typography */
} /* End of temp styles */

.weather-desc { /* Text description for the sky conditions */
  font-size: 20px; /* Legible secondary text size */
  text-transform: capitalize; /* Ensure first letter is capitalized */
  opacity: 0.8; /* Slightly softer than main temperature */
} /* End of description styles */

.weather-stats { /* Lower stats bar for humidity and wind */
  display: flex; /* Horizontal row of statistics */
  gap: 50px; /* Wide spacing between distinct metrics */
  margin-top: 40px; /* Separate from hero section above */
} /* End of stats bar styles */

.stat span { display: block; font-size: 12px; opacity: 0.6; margin-bottom: 5px; } /* Stat label */
.stat strong { font-size: 20px; font-weight: 600; } /* Stat value */

/* Forecast Card: Layout for the scrollable weekly outlook */
.forecast-card { grid-column: span 4; } /* Occupy the remaining grid space in row 1 */

.forecast-scroll { /* Container for horizontal weather items */
  display: flex; /* Align items side-by-side */
  justify-content: space-between; /* Evenly distribute forecast days */
  gap: 15px; /* Spacing between forecast columns */
} /* End of forecast scroll container */

.forecast-item { /* Individual column for a single forecast day */
  display: flex; /* Vertical layout for time, icon, and temp */
  flex-direction: column; /* Stack data points vertically */
  align-items: center; /* Center content within the column */
  gap: 10px; /* Vertical spacing between data points */
} /* End of forecast item styles */

.forecast-time { font-size: 12px; opacity: 0.6; } /* Timestamp label */
.forecast-icon { font-size: 24px; } /* Medium-sized weather emoji */
.forecast-temp { font-weight: 700; font-size: 16px; } /* Predicted temperature value */

/* Financial and News Cards: Shared column-span for the second row */
.exchange-card { grid-column: span 4; } /* Equal width for financial data */
.news-card { grid-column: span 8; } /* Wider card for text-heavy news feed */

.currency-row { /* Style for individual currency exchange lines */
  display: flex; /* Push label and value to opposite ends */
  justify-content: space-between; /* Maximize row space usage */
  padding: 12px 0; /* Vertical spacing between rows */
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05); /* Very subtle separator line */
} /* End of currency row styles */

.news-item { /* Single news article snippet styling */
  margin-bottom: 20px; /* Space between sequential articles */
  padding-bottom: 15px; /* Internal spacing from separator */
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05); /* Divider for readability */
} /* End of news item styles */

.news-title { /* Style for news headlines */
  font-size: 15px; /* Compact size for multiple headlines */
  font-weight: 500; /* Medium weight for readability */
  line-height: 1.4; /* Comfortable line spacing for text */
  margin-bottom: 5px; /* Space between title and source */
} /* End of title styles */

.news-source { font-size: 11px; opacity: 0.5; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; } /* News outlet label */

/* Footer and Loader: Global UI status components */
.glass-footer { /* Minimal bar at the page bottom */
  margin-top: auto; /* Push to the absolute bottom of the container */
  display: flex; /* Align status dot and text horizontally */
  align-items: center; /* Center items vertically */
  gap: 10px; /* Spacing between dot and text */
  padding: 20px; /* Minimal internal padding */
  opacity: 0.6; /* Muted appearance for tertiary info */
} /* End of footer styles */

.status-dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; background: var(--accent); border-radius: 50%; } /* Pulsing live indicator */
.animate-pulse { animation: pulse 2s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite; } /* Keyframe pulse animation */

@keyframes pulse { 0%, 100% { opacity: 1; } 50% { opacity: 0.4; } } /* Pulse keyframe definition */

.loader-overlay { /* Full-screen loading screen appearance */
  height: 100vh; /* Fill full viewport height */
  display: flex; /* Center loader text */
  justify-content: center; /* Horizontal centering */
  align-items: center; /* Vertical centering */
  font-size: 24px; /* Large legible text */
  font-weight: 800; /* Bold weight for impact */
  background: #000; /* Solid black backdrop for loading */
  color: var(--accent); /* Highlighted loader text */
} /* End of loader styles */

resultDisplay.originalContent:
.weather-app {  /* Main container for the entire weather application */
  max-width: 800px;  /* Set the maximum width of the application for desktop users */
  margin: 0 auto;  /* Center the app container horizontally in the browser */
  padding: 2rem;  /* Add internal spacing within the app container */
  text-align: center;  /* Center all text within the weather application */
}  /* End of weather-app selector */

.weather-header h1 {  /* Styling for the application's main header title */
  margin-bottom: 2rem;  /* Add space below the main heading */
  color: #646cff;  /* Set the header text to a brand primary color */
}  /* End of header h1 selector */

.search-form {  /* Styling for the location search form */
  display: flex;  /* Use flexbox for horizontal layout of search elements */
  gap: 1rem;  /* Add spacing between the search input and search button */
  justify-content: center;  /* Center the search form elements horizontally */
  margin-bottom: 3rem;  /* Add significant space below the search bar */
}  /* End of search-form selector */

.search-form input {  /* Styling for the search input field */
  padding: 0.8rem 1.2rem;  /* Set internal spacing for comfortable typing */
  border-radius: 8px;  /* Round the corners of the input box */
  border: 1px solid #444;  /* Add a dark border to match the application theme */
  background: #2a2a2a;  /* Set a dark background for the input field */
  color: white;  /* Ensure input text is white for contrast */
  font-size: 1rem;  /* Set a readable font size for the search text */
  flex: 1;  /* Allow the input to take up available space in the form */
  max-width: 400px;  /* Limit the maximum width of the search input box */
}  /* End of search-form input selector */

.search-form button {  /* Styling for the location search button */
  padding: 0.8rem 1.5rem;  /* Set internal spacing for the search button */
  border-radius: 8px;  /* Round the corners of the search button */
  border: none;  /* Remove default browser button borders */
  background: #646cff;  /* Set the button background to the brand color */
  color: white;  /* Set button text color to white for contrast */
  cursor: pointer;  /* Change cursor to pointer to indicate clickability */
  font-weight: 600;  /* Make the button text slightly bolder */
}  /* End of search-form button selector */

.weather-card {  /* Card-style container for the displayed weather results */
  background: #1a1a1a;  /* Set a dark background color for the weather card */
  padding: 3rem;  /* Add generous internal padding for the weather data */
  border-radius: 16px;  /* Round the card corners more significantly */
  box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4);  /* Add a soft, deep shadow for depth */
}  /* End of weather-card selector */

.main-temp {  /* Styling for the large current temperature display */
  font-size: 5rem;  /* Set a very large font size for the temperature value */
  margin: 1rem 0;  /* Add vertical spacing around the temperature */
  font-weight: 800;  /* Set an extra-bold weight for the temperature text */
}  /* End of main-temp selector */

.weather-desc {  /* Styling for the weather condition description text */
  text-transform: capitalize;  /* Capitalize the first letter of each weather word */
  font-size: 1.5rem;  /* Set a larger font size for the description */
  color: #888;  /* Set a muted gray color for the weather description */
  margin-bottom: 2rem;  /* Add space below the weather description */
}  /* End of weather-desc selector */

.weather-details {  /* Grid container for displaying additional weather metrics */
  display: grid;  /* Use a CSS grid for organized layout of weather details */
  grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);  /* Split the detail section into three equal columns */
  gap: 1.5rem;  /* Add spacing between the individual detail items */
  border-top: 1px solid #333;  /* Add a top divider to separate from main info */
  padding-top: 2rem;  /* Add space inside the top of the detail grid */
}  /* End of weather-details selector */

.detail-item {  /* Container for an individual weather metric (e.g., humidity) */
  display: flex;  /* Use flexbox for vertical stacking of label and value */
  flex-direction: column;  /* Stack items vertically within the detail container */
}  /* End of detail-item selector */

.detail-item span {  /* Styling for the label of a weather metric */
  font-size: 0.9rem;  /* Set a smaller font size for the metric labels */
  color: #777;  /* Set a subtle color for the metric labels */
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;  /* Add space below the metric label */
}  /* End of detail-item span selector */

.astro-info {  /* Grid container for astronomical data like sunrise, sunset, and moon */
  display: grid;  /* Use a grid layout for organization */
  grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);  /* Split into three columns for sunrise, sunset, and moon */
  gap: 1rem;  /* Add internal spacing */
  margin-top: 1.5rem;  /* Add vertical space above the astro section */
  border-top: 1px dashed #444;  /* Add a dashed divider line for visual separation */
  padding-top: 1.5rem;  /* Add internal top padding */
}  /* End of astro-info selector */

.moon-icon {  /* Styling for the calculated shape of the moon */
  font-size: 2.5rem;  /* Make the moon emoji or icon large */
  margin-bottom: 0.2rem;  /* Add minimal space below the moon */
}  /* End of moon-icon selector */

.forecast-section {  /* Container for the 5-day daily forecast display */
  margin-top: 3rem;  /* Add significant space above the forecast area */
}  /* End of forecast-section selector */

.forecast-grid {  /* Grid layout for the daily forecast cards */
  display: grid;  /* Use a grid for consistent layout */
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(140px, 1fr));  /* Automatically adjust card columns */
  gap: 1rem;  /* Add space between forecast days */
}  /* End of forecast-grid selector */

.forecast-day {  /* Styling for an individual day's forecast card */
  background: #1a1a1a;  /* Set a dark background color for the day cards */
  padding: 1rem;  /* Add internal padding for each day */
  border-radius: 12px;  /* Round the corners of the day cards */
  border: 1px solid #333;  /* Add a subtle border */
}  /* End of forecast-day selector */

.forecast-day .day-name {  /* Styling for the day name (e.g., "Monday") */
  color: #646cff;  /* Use the brand primary color for emphasis */
  font-weight: bold;  /* Make the day name bold */
}  /* End of day-name selector */

.forecast-day .day-temp {  /* Styling for the temperature in the forecast card */
  font-size: 1.5rem;  /* Set a large font size for readability */
  margin: 0.5rem 0;  /* Add vertical spacing */
}  /* End of day-temp selector */

/* Global Styles: Basic resets and high-end typography setup */
:root { /* Define global color variables and blur levels */
  --bg-image: url('https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1534088568595-a066f710b81f?auto=format&fit=crop&q=80&w=2000'); /* High-quality atmospheric background */
  --glass-bg: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05); /* Ultra-light translucent background for glass effect */
  --glass-border: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.15); /* Soft border to define glass edges */
  --accent: #00d2ff; /* Primary accent color for highlights and icons */
  --text: #ffffff; /* Default text color for dark background */
} /* End of root variables */

* { /* Apply box-sizing reset to all elements */
  margin: 0; /* Remove default browser margins */
  padding: 0; /* Remove default browser padding */
  box-sizing: border-box; /* Ensure padding doesn't affect element width */
  font-family: 'Inter', -apple-system, sans-serif; /* Use modern, clean sans-serif font stack */
} /* End of universal reset */

body { /* Main body configuration for the full-screen experience */
  background: var(--bg-image) no-repeat center center fixed; /* Apply the fixed atmospheric background */
  background-size: cover; /* Ensure background scales to fill the viewport */
  color: var(--text); /* Set default typography color */
  min-height: 100vh; /* Ensure body takes up at least the full viewport height */
  overflow-x: hidden; /* Prevent horizontal scrollbars during animations */
} /* End of body styles */

/* Application Root Layout: Flexbox container for full-page centering */
.dashboard-root { /* Main wrapper for the entire dashboard UI */
  padding: 40px; /* Internal spacing from screen edges */
  display: flex; /* Use flexbox for vertical orientation */
  flex-direction: column; /* Stack header and main content vertically */
  gap: 30px; /* Consistent spacing between layout blocks */
  max-width: 1400px; /* Limit dashboard width on large monitors */
  margin: 0 auto; /* Center the dashboard horizontally */
} /* End of root container */

/* Glass Header Styles: Translucent navigation bar with search */
.glass-header { /* Header container with glassmorphism properties */
  display: flex; /* Flexbox for logo and search bar alignment */

  -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(20px); /* Safari support for backdrop blur */
  border-radius: 20px; /* Rounded corners for a modern look */
  border: 1px solid var(--glass-border); /* Subtle edge definition */
} /* End of header styles */

.logo-text { /* Application branding text styling */
  font-size: 24px; /* Medium-large font size for prominence */
  font-weight: 800; /* Extra bold weight for brand impact */
  letter-spacing: -1px; /* Tighter letter spacing for a sleek look */

  -webkit-background-clip: text; /* Clip gradient to the text glyphs */

} /* End of logo styles */

/* Search Bar Components: Modern inputs and glass buttons */

  display: flex; /* Inline layout for input and button */
  gap: 10px; /* Spacing between input and search button */
} /* End of search bar container */

.glass-input { /* Style for the city lookup text field */
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); /* Slightly more opaque background for readability */
  border: 1px solid var(--glass-border); /* Subtle border for input box */
  padding: 12px 20px; /* Internal spacing for typed text */
  border-radius: 12px; /* Smooth rounded corners */
  color: white; /* Typed text color */
  outline: none; /* Remove default browser focus ring */
  width: 250px; /* Fixed width for consistent alignment */
} /* End of input styles */

.glass-button { /* Style for the update/submit button */
  background: var(--accent); /* Solid accent color for call-to-action */
  color: #000; /* Dark text for contrast against bright accent */
  border: none; /* Remove default button border */

  cursor: pointer; /* Change cursor to pointer on hover */
  transition: transform 0.2s; /* Smooth hover interaction effect */
} /* End of button styles */

.glass-button:hover { transform: scale(1.05); } /* Slightly enlarge button on hover */

/* Bento Grid System: Modern masonry-style layout for cards */
.bento-grid { /* Main container for all information cards */
  display: grid; /* Enable CSS Grid layout */
  grid-template-columns: repeat(12, 1fr); /* 12-column system for maximum flexibility */

} /* End of grid container */

  background: var(--glass-bg); /* Translucent backdrop */

} /* End of shared card styles */

.section-title { /* Heading styles for card sub-sections */

/* Main Weather Card: Feature module for current conditions */
.main-weather-card { /* Highlighted card for current weather */
  grid-column: span 8; /* Occupy two-thirds of the grid width */
  display: flex; /* Flex layout for hero and stats */
  flex-direction: column; /* Stack hero and stats vertically */
  justify-content: space-between; /* Maximize vertical space usage */
} /* End of main card styles */

.weather-hero { /* High-impact area for temp and icon */
  display: flex; /* Align icon and temperature horizontally */
  align-items: center; /* Center items vertically */
  gap: 40px; /* Wide gap for clear visual separation */
} /* End of hero container */

.big-icon { font-size: 80px; } /* Extra large emoji icon for current weather */

.current-temp { /* Large temperature display styling */
  font-size: 100px; /* Massive size for immediate readability */
  font-weight: 800; /* Extra heavy weight */
  letter-spacing: -4px; /* Tight tracking for high-impact typography */
} /* End of temp styles */

.weather-desc { /* Text description for the sky conditions */
  font-size: 20px; /* Legible secondary text size */
  text-transform: capitalize; /* Ensure first letter is capitalized */
  opacity: 0.8; /* Slightly softer than main temperature */
} /* End of description styles */

.weather-stats { /* Lower stats bar for humidity and wind */
  display: flex; /* Horizontal row of statistics */
  gap: 50px; /* Wide spacing between distinct metrics */
  margin-top: 40px; /* Separate from hero section above */
} /* End of stats bar styles */

.stat span { display: block; font-size: 12px; opacity: 0.6; margin-bottom: 5px; } /* Stat label */
.stat strong { font-size: 20px; font-weight: 600; } /* Stat value */

/* Forecast Card: Layout for the scrollable weekly outlook */
.forecast-card { grid-column: span 4; } /* Occupy the remaining grid space in row 1 */

.forecast-scroll { /* Container for horizontal weather items */
  display: flex; /* Align items side-by-side */
  justify-content: space-between; /* Evenly distribute forecast days */
  gap: 15px; /* Spacing between forecast columns */
} /* End of forecast scroll container */

.forecast-item { /* Individual column for a single forecast day */
  display: flex; /* Vertical layout for time, icon, and temp */
  flex-direction: column; /* Stack data points vertically */
  align-items: center; /* Center content within the column */
  gap: 10px; /* Vertical spacing between data points */
} /* End of forecast item styles */

.forecast-time { font-size: 12px; opacity: 0.6; } /* Timestamp label */
.forecast-icon { font-size: 24px; } /* Medium-sized weather emoji */
.forecast-temp { font-weight: 700; font-size: 16px; } /* Predicted temperature value */

/* Financial and News Cards: Shared column-span for the second row */
.exchange-card { grid-column: span 4; } /* Equal width for financial data */
.news-card { grid-column: span 8; } /* Wider card for text-heavy news feed */

.currency-row { /* Style for individual currency exchange lines */
  display: flex; /* Push label and value to opposite ends */
  justify-content: space-between; /* Maximize row space usage */
  padding: 12px 0; /* Vertical spacing between rows */
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05); /* Very subtle separator line */
} /* End of currency row styles */

.news-item { /* Single news article snippet styling */
  margin-bottom: 20px; /* Space between sequential articles */
  padding-bottom: 15px; /* Internal spacing from separator */
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05); /* Divider for readability */
} /* End of news item styles */

.news-title { /* Style for news headlines */
  font-size: 15px; /* Compact size for multiple headlines */
  font-weight: 500; /* Medium weight for readability */
  line-height: 1.4; /* Comfortable line spacing for text */
  margin-bottom: 5px; /* Space between title and source */
} /* End of title styles */

.news-source { font-size: 11px; opacity: 0.5; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; } /* News outlet label */

/* Footer and Loader: Global UI status components */
.glass-footer { /* Minimal bar at the page bottom */
  margin-top: auto; /* Push to the absolute bottom of the container */
  display: flex; /* Align status dot and text horizontally */
  align-items: center; /* Center items vertically */
  gap: 10px; /* Spacing between dot and text */
  padding: 20px; /* Minimal internal padding */
  opacity: 0.6; /* Muted appearance for tertiary info */
} /* End of footer styles */

.status-dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; background: var(--accent); border-radius: 50%; } /* Pulsing live indicator */
.animate-pulse { animation: pulse 2s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite; } /* Keyframe pulse animation */

@keyframes pulse { 0%, 100% { opacity: 1; } 50% { opacity: 0.4; } } /* Pulse keyframe definition */

.loader-overlay { /* Full-screen loading screen appearance */
  height: 100vh; /* Fill full viewport height */
  display: flex; /* Center loader text */
  justify-content: center; /* Horizontal centering */
  align-items: center; /* Vertical centering */
  font-size: 24px; /* Large legible text */
  font-weight: 800; /* Bold weight for impact */
  background: #000; /* Solid black backdrop for loading */
  color: var(--accent); /* Highlighted loader text */
} /* End of loader styles */

messages.content:
I've built a modern, high-end Weather Dashboard (`AtmosSync`) for your weather site. It features a **Glassmorphism Bento Grid** design with the following integrated modules:

### **Features Overview**
*   **Real-time Weather:** Displays current temperature, conditions, humidity, and wind speed using your backend API.
*   **5-Day Forecast:** A scrollable horizontal outlook for upcoming weather trends.
*   **World Intel (News):** A live feed of the latest headlines fetched through the `api/news` endpoint.
*   **Currency Hub:** Real-time exchange rates (USD to NGN, EUR, GBP) using the `api/exchange-rate` endpoint.
*   **Live Search:** A functional search bar to sync the entire dashboard to any city in the world.

### **Technical Implementation**
*   **UI/UX:** Implemented using **Vanilla CSS** with backdrop-blur effects, high-quality atmospheric backgrounds, and a responsive grid system.
*   **Efficiency:** Uses `Promise.all` to fetch data from all 4 API endpoints in parallel, ensuring the dashboard loads instantly.
*   **Strict Documentation:** As per your request, **every single line of code** (both in `App.tsx` and `App.css`) includes a descriptive comment explaining its purpose.

To see it in action, ensure your FastAPI backend is running and then start the weather site:
```bash
cd weather-site
npm run dev
```

**Would you like me to add any specific weather animations (like falling rain or snow effects) based on the current conditions?**

from datetime import datetime  # Import datetime for timestamp conversion and moon phase calculation

def get_moon_phase(date_obj):  # Helper function to calculate moon phase based on a given date
    # Simple moon phase calculation logic (Synodic Month: ~29.53 days)
    # Known New Moon date: Jan 6, 2000
    diff = date_obj - datetime(2000, 1, 6)  # Calculate days elapsed since the reference new moon
    days = diff.days + diff.seconds / 86400.0  # Convert the difference into decimal days
    lunation = (days % 29.530588853) / 29.530588853  # Determine the current point in the 29.53-day cycle
    
    # Map the decimal lunation value to standard moon phase names
    if lunation < 0.06: return "New Moon"  # Phase 1: Near-zero illumination
    if lunation < 0.19: return "Waxing Crescent"  # Phase 2: Growing sliver
    if lunation < 0.31: return "First Quarter"  # Phase 3: Half-illuminated growing
    if lunation < 0.44: return "Waxing Gibbous"  # Phase 4: Almost full growing
    if lunation < 0.56: return "Full Moon"  # Phase 5: Maximum illumination
    if lunation < 0.69: return "Waning Gibbous"  # Phase 6: Almost full shrinking
    if lunation < 0.81: return "Last Quarter"  # Phase 7: Half-illuminated shrinking
    if lunation < 0.94: return "Waning Crescent"  # Phase 8: Shrinking sliver
    return "New Moon"  # Wrap around to the start of the next cycle

@app.get("/weather")  # Define a GET endpoint for current weather data
def get_weather(city: str = None, lat: float = None, lon: float = None):  # Handler for weather requests
    if lat is not None and lon is not None:  # Check if geographic coordinates are provided
        url = f"http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?lat={lat}&lon={lon}&appid={OPENWEATHER_API_KEY}&units=metric"  # Construct URL with coordinates
    else:  # Default to city name lookup if coordinates are missing
        city = city or "Lagos"  # Default to Lagos if no input is provided
        url = f"http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?q={city}&appid={OPENWEATHER_API_KEY}&units=metric"  # Construct URL with city name
    
    response = requests.get(url).json()  # Send the request and parse the JSON result immediately
    
    # Extract sunrise and sunset from the API response (OpenWeather provides these as UNIX timestamps)
    sunrise_ts = response.get('sys', {}).get('sunrise')  # Get the raw sunrise timestamp
    sunset_ts = response.get('sys', {}).get('sunset')  # Get the raw sunset timestamp
    
    # Generate the descriptive moon status for the current date and location
    current_date = datetime.now()  # Get the current system date and time
    phase = get_moon_phase(current_date)  # Calculate the moon phase for today
    response['astro'] = {  # Add a custom 'astro' object to the response payload
        'sunrise': datetime.fromtimestamp(sunrise_ts).strftime('%H:%M') if sunrise_ts else 'N/A',  # Format sunrise as HH:MM
        'sunset': datetime.fromtimestamp(sunset_ts).strftime('%H:%M') if sunset_ts else 'N/A',  # Format sunset as HH:MM
        'moon_phase': phase,  # Include the calculated moon phase name
        'moon_status': f"Today, {current_date.strftime('%A, %B %d, %Y')}, the Moon is in its {phase} phase. As seen from {city or 'your location'}, it is currently traversing the sky."  # Full descriptive string for UI
    }
    
    return response  # Return the enriched weather and astronomical data

def get_moon_phase(date_obj):  # Helper function to calculate moon phase based on a given date
    # Simple moon phase calculation logic (Synodic Month: ~29.53 days)
    # Known New Moon date: Jan 6, 2000
    diff = date_obj - datetime(2000, 1, 6)  # Calculate days elapsed since the reference new moon
    days = diff.days + diff.seconds / 86400.0  # Convert the difference into decimal days
    lunation = (days % 29.530588853) / 29.530588853  # Determine the current point in the 29.53-day cycle
    
    # Map the decimal lunation value to standard moon phase names
    if lunation < 0.06: return "New Moon"  # Phase 1: Near-zero illumination
    if lunation < 0.19: return "Waxing Crescent"  # Phase 2: Growing sliver
    if lunation < 0.31: return "First Quarter"  # Phase 3: Half-illuminated growing
    if lunation < 0.44: return "Waxing Gibbous"  # Phase 4: Almost full growing
    if lunation < 0.56: return "Full Moon"  # Phase 5: Maximum illumination
    if lunation < 0.69: return "Waning Gibbous"  # Phase 6: Almost full shrinking
    if lunation < 0.81: return "Last Quarter"  # Phase 7: Half-illuminated shrinking
    if lunation < 0.94: return "Waning Crescent"  # Phase 8: Shrinking sliver
    return "New Moon"  # Wrap around to the start of the next cycle

@app.get("/weather")  # Define a GET route to retrieve current weather conditions
def get_weather(city: str = None, lat: float = None, lon: float = None):  # Handler for weather lookups
    if lat is not None and lon is not None:  # Check if geographic coordinates are provided
        url = f"http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?lat={lat}&lon={lon}&appid={OPENWEATHER_API_KEY}&units=metric"  # API URL with coords
    else:  # Default to city name lookup if coordinates are absent
        city = city or "Lagos"  # Use Lagos as the default city if none is specified
        url = f"http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?q={city}&appid={OPENWEATHER_API_KEY}&units=metric"  # API URL with city name
    
    raw_response = requests.get(url).json()  # Send the synchronous GET request and parse JSON
    
    # Extract sunrise and sunset from the API response (OpenWeather provides these as UNIX timestamps)
    sunrise_ts = raw_response.get('sys', {}).get('sunrise')  # Get the raw sunrise timestamp
    sunset_ts = raw_response.get('sys', {}).get('sunset')  # Get the raw sunset timestamp
    
    # Generate the descriptive moon status for the current date and location
    current_date = datetime.now()  # Get the current system date and time
    phase = get_moon_phase(current_date)  # Calculate the moon phase for today
    raw_response['astro'] = {  # Add a custom 'astro' object to the response payload
        'sunrise': datetime.fromtimestamp(sunrise_ts).strftime('%H:%M') if sunrise_ts else 'N/A',  # Format sunrise as HH:MM
        'sunset': datetime.fromtimestamp(sunset_ts).strftime('%H:%M') if sunset_ts else 'N/A',  # Format sunset as HH:MM
        'moon_phase': phase,  # Include the calculated moon phase name
        'moon_status': f"Today, {current_date.strftime('%A, %B %d, %Y')}, the Moon is in its {phase} phase. As seen from {city or 'Lagos'}, it is currently traversing the sky."  # Full descriptive string for UI
    }
    
    return raw_response  # Return the enriched weather and astronomical data payload

import os  # Import the os module to access environment variables and handle file paths
import requests  # Import the requests library to make HTTP requests to external APIs
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Depends, Request  # Import core components and Request for session handling
from fastapi.responses import RedirectResponse  # Import RedirectResponse for browser redirection after auth
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware  # Import CORS middleware for cross-origin resource sharing
from pydantic import BaseModel  # Import BaseModel for data validation and request schemas
from authlib.integrations.starlette_client import OAuth  # Import Authlib client for OAuth2 flow management
from starlette.middleware.sessions import SessionMiddleware  # Import middleware for session-based state storage
from dotenv import load_dotenv  # Import load_dotenv to read configuration from .env files

# Import Music Transfer Services
from services.spotify_service import SpotifyService  # Custom service for Spotify data extraction logic
from services.ytmusic_service import YTMusicService  # Custom service for YouTube Music playlist creation

load_dotenv()  # Load all environment variables defined in the .env file into the system environment

app = FastAPI()  # Initialize the main FastAPI application instance

# Add CORS middleware to allow the React frontend to communicate with this backend securely
app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,  # Use the standard CORSMiddleware class from FastAPI
    allow_origins=["*"],  # Allow requests from any origin during the development phase
    allow_credentials=True,  # Allow browser credentials like cookies to be sent with requests
    allow_methods=["*"],  # Allow all standard HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)
    allow_headers=["*"],  # Allow all custom and standard HTTP headers in requests
)

# Initialize OAuth registry for managing multiple external authentication providers
oauth = OAuth()  # Create an instance of the OAuth class to register our auth providers

# Register Google OAuth provider with necessary endpoints and identity scopes
oauth.register(
    name='google',  # Unique name used to reference the Google authentication provider
    client_id=os.getenv("GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID"),  # Retrieve the Google Client ID from environment variables
    client_secret=os.getenv("GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET"),  # Retrieve the Google Client Secret from environment variables
    server_metadata_url='https://accounts.google.com/.well-known/openid-configuration',  # Discovery URL for Google's OAuth2 configuration
    client_kwargs={'scope': 'openid email profile'}  # Request standard identity scopes from the user
)

# Register Spotify OAuth provider for accessing user library and playlists
oauth.register(
    name='spotify',  # Unique name used to reference the Spotify music service provider
    client_id=os.getenv("SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID"),  # Retrieve the Spotify Client ID from environment variables
    client_secret=os.getenv("SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET"),  # Retrieve the Spotify Client Secret from environment variables
    access_token_url='https://accounts.spotify.com/api/token',  # Spotify's endpoint to exchange codes for tokens
    authorize_url='https://accounts.spotify.com/authorize',  # Spotify's user authorization page URL
    api_base_url='https://api.spotify.com/v1/',  # Base URL for making authenticated Spotify API calls
    client_kwargs={'scope': 'user-library-read playlist-read-private'}  # Scopes required to read user data
)

# Register Apple OAuth provider as a placeholder for future account integration
oauth.register(
    name='apple',  # Unique name used to reference the Apple authentication service
    client_id=os.getenv("APPLE_CLIENT_ID"),  # Retrieve the Apple Client ID from environment variables
    client_secret=os.getenv("APPLE_CLIENT_SECRET"),  # Retrieve the Apple Client Secret from environment variables
    authorize_url='https://appleid.apple.com/auth/authorize',  # Apple's standard authorization endpoint
    access_token_url='https://appleid.apple.com/auth/token'  # Apple's standard token exchange endpoint
)

# Add Session Middleware to the FastAPI application to enable encrypted state persistence
app.add_middleware(SessionMiddleware, secret_key=os.getenv("SESSION_SECRET", "super-secret-key"))  # Required for OAuth handshake security

# Load API keys and global credentials from environment variables for external services
OPENWEATHER_API_KEY = os.getenv("OPENWEATHER_API_KEY", "YOUR_OPENWEATHER_KEY")  # Weather data provider key
NEWS_API_KEY = os.getenv("NEWS_API_KEY", "YOUR_NEWS_API_KEY")  # Global and local news provider key
EXCHANGE_RATE_API_KEY = os.getenv("EXCHANGE_RATE_API_KEY", "YOUR_EXCHANGE_RATE_KEY")  # Currency conversion key

# Re-fetch Spotify credentials for direct service initialization if needed
SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID = os.getenv("SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID")  # Fetch Spotify ID again for logic below
SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET = os.getenv("SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET")  # Fetch Spotify Secret for logic below
SPOTIFY_REDIRECT_URI = os.getenv("SPOTIFY_REDIRECT_URI")  # Fetch Spotify Redirect URI for logic below

# Define the location of the YouTube Music authentication JSON file
YT_MUSIC_AUTH_FILE = os.getenv("YT_MUSIC_AUTH_FILE", "oauth.json")  # Default to oauth.json if not specified

# Define the data structure for music transfer requests using Pydantic
class TransferRequest(BaseModel):  # Inherit from BaseModel for automatic validation
    spotify_playlist_id: str  # Required field: The ID of the Spotify playlist to migrate
    target_playlist_name: str = "Transferred from Spotify"  # Optional field: Name for the new YTM playlist

@app.get("/login/{provider}")  # Define a GET route to initiate the OAuth login process
async def login(provider: str, request: Request):  # Asynchronous handler for starting the login flow
    if provider not in ['google', 'spotify', 'apple']:  # Validate that the requested service is supported
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Unsupported provider")  # Return an error for unknown providers
    redirect_uri = f"http://localhost:8000/auth/{provider}"  # Construct the absolute callback URL for the provider
    return await oauth.create_client(provider).authorize_redirect(request, redirect_uri)  # Redirect user to provider's login

@app.get("/auth/{provider}")  # Define a GET route to handle the callback from the OAuth provider
async def auth_callback(provider: str, request: Request):  # Asynchronous handler for processing the provider's response
    client = oauth.create_client(provider)  # Create a specific client instance for the given provider
    token = await client.authorize_access_token(request)  # Exchange the auth code for a valid access token
    user = token.get('userinfo')  # Extract the user's profile information from the token payload
    if user:  # Check if user information was successfully retrieved
        request.session['user'] = user  # Store the user's profile data in the secure session cookie
    return RedirectResponse(url='http://localhost:3000/dashboard')  # Redirect the browser back to the React dashboard

@app.post("/transfer")  # Define a POST endpoint to handle the music playlist migration logic
def transfer_music(request: TransferRequest):  # Synchronous handler for executing the track-by-track transfer
    try:  # Wrap the execution in a try block to catch and handle API or logic errors
        # Perform mandatory validation on Spotify application credentials
        if not SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID or SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID == "YOUR_SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID":  # Check for default or missing ID
            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Spotify Client ID is not configured in .env")  # Abort if invalid
        if not SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET or SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET == "YOUR_SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET":  # Check for missing secret
            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Spotify Client Secret is not configured in .env")  # Abort if invalid
        
        # Verify the existence of the YouTube Music authentication file on the local filesystem
        if not os.path.exists(YT_MUSIC_AUTH_FILE):  # Check if the required JSON file exists
             raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=f"YTMusic auth file '{YT_MUSIC_AUTH_FILE}' not found")  # Abort if missing

        # Initialize the Spotify service with the validated application credentials
        spotify = SpotifyService(
            client_id=SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID,  # Pass the client ID to the service constructor
            client_secret=SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET,  # Pass the client secret to the service constructor
            redirect_uri=SPOTIFY_REDIRECT_URI  # Pass the redirect URI to the service constructor
        )
        # Initialize the YouTube Music service using the specified authentication file
        ytm = YTMusicService(YT_MUSIC_AUTH_FILE)  # Pass the file path to the YTM service constructor

        # Step 1: Request track metadata from the Spotify API for the given playlist ID
        print(f"Fetching tracks from Spotify: {request.spotify_playlist_id}")  # Log the source ID for debugging
        tracks = spotify.get_playlist_tracks(request.spotify_playlist_id)  # Execute the extraction logic
        
        if not tracks:  # Check if the playlist was empty or inaccessible
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="No tracks found in the provided Spotify playlist")  # Notify user

        # Step 2: Use the parallelized search engine to reconstruct the playlist on YouTube Music
        print(f"Transferring {len(tracks)} tracks to YouTube Music...")  # Log the track count for progress tracking
        playlist_id = ytm.create_playlist(  # Execute the migration and creation logic
            name=request.target_playlist_name,  # Use the user-provided or default playlist name
            description="A premium playlist migrated using BridgeBeat.",  # Set a standard description
            tracks=tracks  # Provide the list of tracks extracted from Spotify
        )

        return {  # Return a JSON object summarizing the successful migration
            "status": "success",  # Set the overall status to success
            "tracks_moved": len(tracks),  # Report the total number of tracks processed
            "ytm_playlist_id": playlist_id  # Provide the ID of the newly created YTM playlist
        }
    except HTTPException as he:  # Catch and re-raise specifically defined HTTP exceptions
        raise he  # Re-throw the exception to be handled by FastAPI's error handlers
    except Exception as e:  # Catch any unexpected runtime or connection errors
        print(f"Transfer Error: {str(e)}")  # Log the error details to the server console
        if "http status: 404" in str(e).lower():  # Specifically handle common Spotify 404 errors
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Spotify playlist not found. Check the ID.")  # User-friendly error
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))  # Return a generic internal server error for other cases

@app.get("/weather")  # Define a GET route to retrieve current weather conditions
def get_weather(city: str = None, lat: float = None, lon: float = None):  # Handler for weather lookups
    if lat is not None and lon is not None:  # Check if geographic coordinates are provided
        url = f"http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?lat={lat}&lon={lon}&appid={OPENWEATHER_API_KEY}&units=metric"  # API URL with coords
    else:  # Default to city name lookup if coordinates are absent
        city = city or "Lagos"  # Use Lagos as the default city if none is specified
        url = f"http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?q={city}&appid={OPENWEATHER_API_KEY}&units=metric"  # API URL with city name
    response = requests.get(url)  # Send the synchronous GET request to OpenWeatherMap
    return response.json()  # Return the weather data payload as JSON

@app.get("/forecast")  # Define a GET route for obtaining a 5-day weather forecast
def get_forecast(city: str = None, lat: float = None, lon: float = None):  # Handler for forecast lookups
    if lat is not None and lon is not None:  # Check for latitude and longitude presence
        url = f"http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/forecast?lat={lat}&lon={lon}&appid={OPENWEATHER_API_KEY}&units=metric"  # URL with coords
    else:  # Fallback to city name for forecast data
        city = city or "Lagos"  # Use Lagos as the default city name
        url = f"http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/forecast?q={city}&appid={OPENWEATHER_API_KEY}&units=metric"  # URL with city name
    response = requests.get(url)  # Execute the GET request to fetch forecast data
    return response.json()  # Return the detailed forecast data as JSON

@app.get("/news")  # Define a GET route for retrieving filtered news headlines
def get_news(query: str = "Lagos -occult -ritual -cult -killing"):  # Handler for news queries with default filtering
    url = f"https://newsapi.org/v2/everything?q={query}&sortBy=relevancy&language=en&apiKey={NEWS_API_KEY}"  # Construct filtered NewsAPI URL
    response = requests.get(url)  # Fetch news stories matching the query parameters
    return response.json()  # Return the list of news articles as JSON

@app.get("/exchange-rate")  # Define a GET route to check currency exchange rates
def get_exchange_rate(base: str = "USD"):  # Handler for exchange rate inquiries with a base currency
    url = f"https://v6.exchangerate-api.com/v6/{EXCHANGE_RATE_API_KEY}/latest/{base}"  # API URL for current rates
    response = requests.get(url)  # Send the request to the ExchangeRate-API service
    return response.json()  # Return the currency data payload as JSON

if __name__ == "__main__":  # Ensure the application only runs when executed directly as a script
    import uvicorn  # Import the uvicorn ASGI server locally
    uvicorn.run(app, host="0.0.0.0", port=8000)  # Start the web server on all interfaces at port 8000

from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Depends, Request  # Import core components and Request for session handling
from fastapi.responses import RedirectResponse  # Import RedirectResponse for browser redirection after auth
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware  # Import CORS middleware for cross-origin resource sharing
from pydantic import BaseModel  # Import BaseModel for data validation and request schemas
from authlib.integrations.starlette_client import OAuth  # Import Authlib client for OAuth2 flow management
from starlette.middleware.sessions import SessionMiddleware  # Import middleware for session-based state storage
from dotenv import load_dotenv  # Import load_dotenv to read configuration from .env files

# Import Music Transfer Services

    CORSMiddleware,  # Use the standard CORSMiddleware class from FastAPI
    allow_origins=["*"],  # Allow requests from any origin during the development phase
    allow_credentials=True,  # Allow browser credentials like cookies to be sent with requests
    allow_methods=["*"],  # Allow all standard HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)
    allow_headers=["*"],  # Allow all custom and standard HTTP headers in requests
)

# Initialize OAuth registry for managing multiple external authentication providers

    name='google',  # Unique name used to reference the Google authentication provider
    client_id=os.getenv("GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID"),  # Retrieve the Google Client ID from environment variables
    client_secret=os.getenv("GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET"),  # Retrieve the Google Client Secret from environment variables
    server_metadata_url='https://accounts.google.com/.well-known/openid-configuration',  # Discovery URL for Google's OAuth2 configuration
    client_kwargs={'scope': 'openid email profile'}  # Request standard identity scopes from the user
)

# Register Spotify OAuth provider for accessing user library and playlists
oauth.register(
    name='spotify',  # Unique name used to reference the Spotify music service provider
    client_id=os.getenv("SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID"),  # Retrieve the Spotify Client ID from environment variables
    client_secret=os.getenv("SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET"),  # Retrieve the Spotify Client Secret from environment variables
    access_token_url='https://accounts.spotify.com/api/token',  # Spotify's endpoint to exchange codes for tokens
    authorize_url='https://accounts.spotify.com/authorize',  # Spotify's user authorization page URL
    api_base_url='https://api.spotify.com/v1/',  # Base URL for making authenticated Spotify API calls
    client_kwargs={'scope': 'user-library-read playlist-read-private'}  # Scopes required to read user data
)

# Register Apple OAuth provider as a placeholder for future account integration
oauth.register(
    name='apple',  # Unique name used to reference the Apple authentication service
    client_id=os.getenv("APPLE_CLIENT_ID"),  # Retrieve the Apple Client ID from environment variables
    client_secret=os.getenv("APPLE_CLIENT_SECRET"),  # Retrieve the Apple Client Secret from environment variables
    authorize_url='https://appleid.apple.com/auth/authorize',  # Apple's standard authorization endpoint
    access_token_url='https://appleid.apple.com/auth/token'  # Apple's standard token exchange endpoint
)

# Add Session Middleware to the FastAPI application to enable encrypted state persistence
app.add_middleware(SessionMiddleware, secret_key=os.getenv("SESSION_SECRET", "super-secret-key"))  # Required for OAuth handshake security

# Load API keys and global credentials from environment variables for external services
OPENWEATHER_API_KEY = os.getenv("OPENWEATHER_API_KEY", "YOUR_OPENWEATHER_KEY")  # Weather data provider key
NEWS_API_KEY = os.getenv("NEWS_API_KEY", "YOUR_NEWS_API_KEY")  # Global and local news provider key
EXCHANGE_RATE_API_KEY = os.getenv("EXCHANGE_RATE_API_KEY", "YOUR_EXCHANGE_RATE_KEY")  # Currency conversion key

# Re-fetch Spotify credentials for direct service initialization if needed
SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID = os.getenv("SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID")  # Fetch Spotify ID again for logic below
SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET = os.getenv("SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET")  # Fetch Spotify Secret for logic below
SPOTIFY_REDIRECT_URI = os.getenv("SPOTIFY_REDIRECT_URI")  # Fetch Spotify Redirect URI for logic below

# Define the location of the YouTube Music authentication JSON file
YT_MUSIC_AUTH_FILE = os.getenv("YT_MUSIC_AUTH_FILE", "oauth.json")  # Default to oauth.json if not specified

# Define the data structure for music transfer requests using Pydantic
class TransferRequest(BaseModel):  # Inherit from BaseModel for automatic validation
    spotify_playlist_id: str  # Required field: The ID of the Spotify playlist to migrate
    target_playlist_name: str = "Transferred from Spotify"  # Optional field: Name for the new YTM playlist

@app.get("/login/{provider}")  # Define a GET route to initiate the OAuth login process
async def login(provider: str, request: Request):  # Asynchronous handler for starting the login flow
    if provider not in ['google', 'spotify', 'apple']:  # Validate that the requested service is supported
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Unsupported provider")  # Return an error for unknown providers
    redirect_uri = f"http://localhost:8000/auth/{provider}"  # Construct the absolute callback URL for the provider
    return await oauth.create_client(provider).authorize_redirect(request, redirect_uri)  # Redirect user to provider's login

@app.get("/auth/{provider}")  # Define a GET route to handle the callback from the OAuth provider
async def auth_callback(provider: str, request: Request):  # Asynchronous handler for processing the provider's response
    client = oauth.create_client(provider)  # Create a specific client instance for the given provider
    token = await client.authorize_access_token(request)  # Exchange the auth code for a valid access token
    user = token.get('userinfo')  # Extract the user's profile information from the token payload
    if user:  # Check if user information was successfully retrieved
        request.session['user'] = user  # Store the user's profile data in the secure session cookie
    return RedirectResponse(url='http://localhost:3000/dashboard')  # Redirect the browser back to the React dashboard

@app.post("/transfer")  # Define a POST endpoint to handle the music playlist migration logic
def transfer_music(request: TransferRequest):  # Synchronous handler for executing the track-by-track transfer
    try:  # Wrap the execution in a try block to catch and handle API or logic errors
        # Perform mandatory validation on Spotify application credentials
        if not SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID or SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID == "YOUR_SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID":  # Check for default or missing ID
            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Spotify Client ID is not configured in .env")  # Abort if invalid
        if not SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET or SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET == "YOUR_SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET":  # Check for missing secret
            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Spotify Client Secret is not configured in .env")  # Abort if invalid
        
        # Verify the existence of the YouTube Music authentication file on the local filesystem
        if not os.path.exists(YT_MUSIC_AUTH_FILE):  # Check if the required JSON file exists
             raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=f"YTMusic auth file '{YT_MUSIC_AUTH_FILE}' not found")  # Abort if missing

        # Initialize the Spotify service with the validated application credentials
        spotify = SpotifyService(
            client_id=SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID,  # Pass the client ID to the service constructor
            client_secret=SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET,  # Pass the client secret to the service constructor
            redirect_uri=SPOTIFY_REDIRECT_URI  # Pass the redirect URI to the service constructor
        )
        # Initialize the YouTube Music service using the specified authentication file
        ytm = YTMusicService(YT_MUSIC_AUTH_FILE)  # Pass the file path to the YTM service constructor

        # Step 1: Request track metadata from the Spotify API for the given playlist ID
        print(f"Fetching tracks from Spotify: {request.spotify_playlist_id}")  # Log the source ID for debugging
        tracks = spotify.get_playlist_tracks(request.spotify_playlist_id)  # Execute the extraction logic
        
        if not tracks:  # Check if the playlist was empty or inaccessible
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="No tracks found in the provided Spotify playlist")  # Notify user

        # Step 2: Use the parallelized search engine to reconstruct the playlist on YouTube Music
        print(f"Transferring {len(tracks)} tracks to YouTube Music...")  # Log the track count for progress tracking
        playlist_id = ytm.create_playlist(  # Execute the migration and creation logic
            name=request.target_playlist_name,  # Use the user-provided or default playlist name
            description="A premium playlist migrated using BridgeBeat.",  # Set a standard description
            tracks=tracks  # Provide the list of tracks extracted from Spotify
        )

        return {  # Return a JSON object summarizing the successful migration
            "status": "success",  # Set the overall status to success
            "tracks_moved": len(tracks),  # Report the total number of tracks processed
            "ytm_playlist_id": playlist_id  # Provide the ID of the newly created YTM playlist
        }
    except HTTPException as he:  # Catch and re-raise specifically defined HTTP exceptions
        raise he  # Re-throw the exception to be handled by FastAPI's error handlers
    except Exception as e:  # Catch any unexpected runtime or connection errors
        print(f"Transfer Error: {str(e)}")  # Log the error details to the server console
        if "http status: 404" in str(e).lower():  # Specifically handle common Spotify 404 errors
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Spotify playlist not found. Check the ID.")  # User-friendly error
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))  # Return a generic internal server error for other cases

def get_moon_phase(date_obj):  # Helper function to calculate moon phase based on a given date
    # Simple moon phase calculation logic (Synodic Month: ~29.53 days)
    # Known New Moon date: Jan 6, 2000
    diff = date_obj - datetime(2000, 1, 6)  # Calculate days elapsed since the reference new moon
    days = diff.days + diff.seconds / 86400.0  # Convert the difference into decimal days
    lunation = (days % 29.530588853) / 29.530588853  # Determine the current point in the 29.53-day cycle
    
    # Map the decimal lunation value to standard moon phase names
    if lunation < 0.06: return "New Moon"  # Phase 1: Near-zero illumination
    if lunation < 0.19: return "Waxing Crescent"  # Phase 2: Growing sliver
    if lunation < 0.31: return "First Quarter"  # Phase 3: Half-illuminated growing
    if lunation < 0.44: return "Waxing Gibbous"  # Phase 4: Almost full growing
    if lunation < 0.56: return "Full Moon"  # Phase 5: Maximum illumination
    if lunation < 0.69: return "Waning Gibbous"  # Phase 6: Almost full shrinking
    if lunation < 0.81: return "Last Quarter"  # Phase 7: Half-illuminated shrinking
    if lunation < 0.94: return "Waning Crescent"  # Phase 8: Shrinking sliver
    return "New Moon"  # Wrap around to the start of the next cycle

@app.get("/weather")  # Define a GET route to retrieve current weather conditions
def get_weather(city: str = None, lat: float = None, lon: float = None):  # Handler for weather lookups
    if lat is not None and lon is not None:  # Check if geographic coordinates are provided
        url = f"http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?lat={lat}&lon={lon}&appid={OPENWEATHER_API_KEY}&units=metric"  # API URL with coords
    else:  # Default to city name lookup if coordinates are absent
        city = city or "Lagos"  # Use Lagos as the default city if none is specified
        url = f"http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?q={city}&appid={OPENWEATHER_API_KEY}&units=metric"  # API URL with city name
    
    raw_response = requests.get(url).json()  # Send the synchronous GET request and parse JSON
    
    # Extract sunrise and sunset from the API response (OpenWeather provides these as UNIX timestamps)
    sunrise_ts = raw_response.get('sys', {}).get('sunrise')  # Get the raw sunrise timestamp
    sunset_ts = raw_response.get('sys', {}).get('sunset')  # Get the raw sunset timestamp
    
    # Generate the descriptive moon status for the current date and location
    current_date = datetime.now()  # Get the current system date and time
    phase = get_moon_phase(current_date)  # Calculate the moon phase for today
    raw_response['astro'] = {  # Add a custom 'astro' object to the response payload
        'sunrise': datetime.fromtimestamp(sunrise_ts).strftime('%H:%M') if sunrise_ts else 'N/A',  # Format sunrise as HH:MM
        'sunset': datetime.fromtimestamp(sunset_ts).strftime('%H:%M') if sunset_ts else 'N/A',  # Format sunset as HH:MM
        'moon_phase': phase,  # Include the calculated moon phase name
        'moon_status': f"Today, {current_date.strftime('%A, %B %d, %Y')}, the Moon is in its {phase} phase. As seen from {city or 'Lagos'}, it is currently traversing the sky."  # Full descriptive string for UI
    }
    
    return raw_response  # Return the enriched weather and astronomical data payload

@app.get("/forecast")  # Define a GET route for obtaining a 5-day weather forecast
def get_forecast(city: str = None, lat: float = None, lon: float = None):  # Handler for forecast lookups
    if lat is not None and lon is not None:  # Check for latitude and longitude presence
        url = f"http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/forecast?lat={lat}&lon={lon}&appid={OPENWEATHER_API_KEY}&units=metric"  # URL with coords
    else:  # Fallback to city name for forecast data
        city = city or "Lagos"  # Use Lagos as the default city name
        url = f"http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/forecast?q={city}&appid={OPENWEATHER_API_KEY}&units=metric"  # URL with city name
    response = requests.get(url)  # Execute the GET request to fetch forecast data
    return response.json()  # Return the detailed forecast data as JSON

@app.get("/news")  # Define a GET route for retrieving filtered news headlines
def get_news(query: str = "Lagos -occult -ritual -cult -killing"):  # Handler for news queries with default filtering
    url = f"https://newsapi.org/v2/everything?q={query}&sortBy=relevancy&language=en&apiKey={NEWS_API_KEY}"  # Construct filtered NewsAPI URL
    response = requests.get(url)  # Fetch news stories matching the query parameters
    return response.json()  # Return the list of news articles as JSON

@app.get("/exchange-rate")  # Define a GET route to check currency exchange rates
def get_exchange_rate(base: str = "USD"):  # Handler for exchange rate inquiries with a base currency
    url = f"https://v6.exchangerate-api.com/v6/{EXCHANGE_RATE_API_KEY}/latest/{base}"  # API URL for current rates
    response = requests.get(url)  # Send the request to the ExchangeRate-API service
    return response.json()  # Return the currency data payload as JSON

if __name__ == "__main__":  # Ensure the application only runs when executed directly as a script
    import uvicorn  # Import the uvicorn ASGI server locally
    uvicorn.run(app, host="0.0.0.0", port=8000)  # Start the web server on all interfaces at port 8000

resultDisplay.originalContent:
/* Global Styles: Basic resets and high-end typography setup */
:root { /* Define global color variables and blur levels */
  --bg-image: url('https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1534088568595-a066f710b81f?auto=format&fit=crop&q=80&w=2000'); /* High-quality atmospheric background */
  --glass-bg: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05); /* Ultra-light translucent background for glass effect */
  --glass-border: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.15); /* Soft border to define glass edges */
  --accent: #00d2ff; /* Primary accent color for highlights and icons */
  --text: #ffffff; /* Default text color for dark background */
} /* End of root variables */

* { /* Apply box-sizing reset to all elements */
  margin: 0; /* Remove default browser margins */
  padding: 0; /* Remove default browser padding */
  box-sizing: border-box; /* Ensure padding doesn't affect element width */
  font-family: 'Inter', -apple-system, sans-serif; /* Use modern, clean sans-serif font stack */
} /* End of universal reset */

body { /* Main body configuration for the full-screen experience */
  background: var(--bg-image) no-repeat center center fixed; /* Apply the fixed atmospheric background */
  background-size: cover; /* Ensure background scales to fill the viewport */
  color: var(--text); /* Set default typography color */
  min-height: 100vh; /* Ensure body takes up at least the full viewport height */
  overflow-x: hidden; /* Prevent horizontal scrollbars during animations */
} /* End of body styles */

/* Application Root Layout: Flexbox container for full-page centering */
.dashboard-root { /* Main wrapper for the entire dashboard UI */
  padding: 40px; /* Internal spacing from screen edges */
  display: flex; /* Use flexbox for vertical orientation */
  flex-direction: column; /* Stack header and main content vertically */
  gap: 30px; /* Consistent spacing between layout blocks */
  max-width: 1400px; /* Limit dashboard width on large monitors */
  margin: 0 auto; /* Center the dashboard horizontally */
} /* End of root container */

/* Glass Header Styles: Translucent navigation bar with search */
.glass-header { /* Header container with glassmorphism properties */
  display: flex; /* Flexbox for logo and search bar alignment */
  justify-content: space-between; /* Push logo and search to opposite ends */
  align-items: center; /* Vertically center header content */
  padding: 20px 30px; /* Internal padding for header content */
  background: var(--glass-bg); /* Semi-transparent background */
  backdrop-filter: blur(20px); /* Apply sophisticated background blur */
  -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(20px); /* Safari support for backdrop blur */
  border-radius: 20px; /* Rounded corners for a modern look */
  border: 1px solid var(--glass-border); /* Subtle edge definition */
} /* End of header styles */

.logo-text { /* Application branding text styling */
  font-size: 24px; /* Medium-large font size for prominence */
  font-weight: 800; /* Extra bold weight for brand impact */
  letter-spacing: -1px; /* Tighter letter spacing for a sleek look */
  background: linear-gradient(to right, #ffffff, var(--accent)); /* Gradient text effect */
  -webkit-background-clip: text; /* Clip gradient to the text glyphs */
  -webkit-text-fill-color: transparent; /* Make original text color transparent */
} /* End of logo styles */

/* Search Bar Components: Modern inputs and glass buttons */
.search-bar { /* Form container for location input */
  display: flex; /* Inline layout for input and button */
  gap: 10px; /* Spacing between input and search button */
} /* End of search bar container */

.glass-input { /* Style for the city lookup text field */
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); /* Slightly more opaque background for readability */
  border: 1px solid var(--glass-border); /* Subtle border for input box */
  padding: 12px 20px; /* Internal spacing for typed text */
  border-radius: 12px; /* Smooth rounded corners */
  color: white; /* Typed text color */
  outline: none; /* Remove default browser focus ring */
  width: 250px; /* Fixed width for consistent alignment */
} /* End of input styles */

.glass-button { /* Style for the update/submit button */
  background: var(--accent); /* Solid accent color for call-to-action */
  color: #000; /* Dark text for contrast against bright accent */
  border: none; /* Remove default button border */
  padding: 12px 24px; /* Internal padding for button tap area */
  border-radius: 12px; /* Match input corner radius */
  font-weight: 700; /* Bold text for clarity */
  cursor: pointer; /* Change cursor to pointer on hover */
  transition: transform 0.2s; /* Smooth hover interaction effect */
} /* End of button styles */

.glass-button:hover { transform: scale(1.05); } /* Slightly enlarge button on hover */

/* Bento Grid System: Modern masonry-style layout for cards */
.bento-grid { /* Main container for all information cards */
  display: grid; /* Enable CSS Grid layout */
  grid-template-columns: repeat(12, 1fr); /* 12-column system for maximum flexibility */
  grid-template-rows: auto auto; /* Two primary rows of information */
  gap: 20px; /* Consistent spacing between grid items */
} /* End of grid container */

/* Universal Glass Card Properties: Base styling for all dash modules */
.glass-card { /* Shared properties for every dashboard card */
  background: var(--glass-bg); /* Translucent backdrop */
  backdrop-filter: blur(15px); /* Moderate blur for readability */
  -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(15px); /* Safari blur support */
  border-radius: 24px; /* Large, modern corner radii */
  border: 1px solid var(--glass-border); /* Consistent subtle borders */
  padding: 30px; /* Standard internal card padding */
  transition: background 0.3s; /* Smooth transition for interaction states */
} /* End of shared card styles */

.section-title { /* Heading styles for card sub-sections */
  font-size: 14px; /* Small heading for subtle information hierarchy */
  text-transform: uppercase; /* Uppercase for a technical label feel */
  letter-spacing: 2px; /* Wide spacing for better readability at small sizes */
  margin-bottom: 20px; /* Space between title and card content */
  opacity: 0.6; /* Reduced opacity to emphasize data over labels */
} /* End of section title styles */

/* Main Weather Card: Feature module for current conditions */
.main-weather-card { /* Highlighted card for current weather */
  grid-column: span 8; /* Occupy two-thirds of the grid width */
  display: flex; /* Flex layout for hero and stats */
  flex-direction: column; /* Stack hero and stats vertically */
  justify-content: space-between; /* Maximize vertical space usage */
} /* End of main card styles */

.weather-hero { /* High-impact area for temp and icon */
  display: flex; /* Align icon and temperature horizontally */
  align-items: center; /* Center items vertically */
  gap: 40px; /* Wide gap for clear visual separation */
} /* End of hero container */

.big-icon { font-size: 80px; } /* Extra large emoji icon for current weather */

.current-temp { /* Large temperature display styling */
  font-size: 100px; /* Massive size for immediate readability */
  font-weight: 800; /* Extra heavy weight */
  letter-spacing: -4px; /* Tight tracking for high-impact typography */
} /* End of temp styles */

.weather-desc { /* Text description for the sky conditions */
  font-size: 20px; /* Legible secondary text size */
  text-transform: capitalize; /* Ensure first letter is capitalized */
  opacity: 0.8; /* Slightly softer than main temperature */
} /* End of description styles */

.weather-stats { /* Lower stats bar for humidity and wind */
  display: flex; /* Horizontal row of statistics */
  gap: 50px; /* Wide spacing between distinct metrics */
  margin-top: 40px; /* Separate from hero section above */
} /* End of stats bar styles */

.stat span { display: block; font-size: 12px; opacity: 0.6; margin-bottom: 5px; } /* Stat label */
.stat strong { font-size: 20px; font-weight: 600; } /* Stat value */

/* Forecast Card: Layout for the scrollable weekly outlook */
.forecast-card { grid-column: span 4; } /* Occupy the remaining grid space in row 1 */

.forecast-scroll { /* Container for horizontal weather items */
  display: flex; /* Align items side-by-side */
  justify-content: space-between; /* Evenly distribute forecast days */
  gap: 15px; /* Spacing between forecast columns */
} /* End of forecast scroll container */

.forecast-item { /* Individual column for a single forecast day */
  display: flex; /* Vertical layout for time, icon, and temp */
  flex-direction: column; /* Stack data points vertically */
  align-items: center; /* Center content within the column */
  gap: 10px; /* Vertical spacing between data points */
} /* End of forecast item styles */

.forecast-time { font-size: 12px; opacity: 0.6; } /* Timestamp label */
.forecast-icon { font-size: 24px; } /* Medium-sized weather emoji */
.forecast-temp { font-weight: 700; font-size: 16px; } /* Predicted temperature value */

/* Financial and News Cards: Shared column-span for the second row */
.exchange-card { grid-column: span 4; } /* Equal width for financial data */
.news-card { grid-column: span 8; } /* Wider card for text-heavy news feed */

.currency-row { /* Style for individual currency exchange lines */
  display: flex; /* Push label and value to opposite ends */
  justify-content: space-between; /* Maximize row space usage */
  padding: 12px 0; /* Vertical spacing between rows */
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05); /* Very subtle separator line */
} /* End of currency row styles */

.news-item { /* Single news article snippet styling */
  margin-bottom: 20px; /* Space between sequential articles */
  padding-bottom: 15px; /* Internal spacing from separator */
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05); /* Divider for readability */
} /* End of news item styles */

.news-title { /* Style for news headlines */
  font-size: 15px; /* Compact size for multiple headlines */
  font-weight: 500; /* Medium weight for readability */
  line-height: 1.4; /* Comfortable line spacing for text */
  margin-bottom: 5px; /* Space between title and source */
} /* End of title styles */

.news-source { font-size: 11px; opacity: 0.5; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; } /* News outlet label */

/* Footer and Loader: Global UI status components */
.glass-footer { /* Minimal bar at the page bottom */
  margin-top: auto; /* Push to the absolute bottom of the container */
  display: flex; /* Align status dot and text horizontally */
  align-items: center; /* Center items vertically */
  gap: 10px; /* Spacing between dot and text */
  padding: 20px; /* Minimal internal padding */
  opacity: 0.6; /* Muted appearance for tertiary info */
} /* End of footer styles */

.status-dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; background: var(--accent); border-radius: 50%; } /* Pulsing live indicator */
.animate-pulse { animation: pulse 2s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite; } /* Keyframe pulse animation */

@keyframes pulse { 0%, 100% { opacity: 1; } 50% { opacity: 0.4; } } /* Pulse keyframe definition */

.loader-overlay { /* Full-screen loading screen appearance */
  height: 100vh; /* Fill full viewport height */
  display: flex; /* Center loader text */
  justify-content: center; /* Horizontal centering */
  align-items: center; /* Vertical centering */
  font-size: 24px; /* Large legible text */
  font-weight: 800; /* Bold weight for impact */
  background: #000; /* Solid black backdrop for loading */
  color: var(--accent); /* Highlighted loader text */
} /* End of loader styles */
